authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (Stony Ridge Lake, El Dorado Co., not listed among release waters)
USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Reservoir layer record for gaz_id 235552, "Stony Ridge Lake", Reservoir, El Dorado County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":235552,"gaz_name":"Stony Ridge Lake","gaz_featureclass":"Reservoir","fcode":39004,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"El Dorado"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13373809.7486, 4718737.5413) converts to lon -120.138977, lat 38.979514, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-120.13889, 38.97942) to within ~10m.

parameters.note: Stony Ridge Lake is a high alpine waterbody (~2,382 m / ~7,815 ft; carries GNIS feature ID 235552 and is mapped as a reservoir) in the Desolation Wilderness (El Dorado Co.), at the eastern base of Rubicon Peak in the Meeks Creek drainage. It lies within the Lake Tahoe basin (Meeks Creek flows down to Meeks Bay on Lake Tahoe's west shore) but is high up the Meeks chain — Lake Genevieve, Crag Lake, Hidden Lake, Shadow Lake, Stony Ridge Lake, Rubicon Lake — well above the 'first lake' (Lake Genevieve). The special Lake Tahoe tributary closure (§7.50(b)(152), which reaches only flowing Tahoe tributaries upstream to the first lake, and the lake within 300 ft of tributary mouths) therefore does not apply to Stony Ridge Lake itself. The lake is not listed by name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation §5.85(a)(1) — which applies identically to 'lakes, reservoirs, and ponds': open all year, 5/10. Stony Ridge Lake is not in CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document, and no current authoritative source names a fish population here, so species and stocking are intentionally left empty; the season/bag/license rows below state what governs the lake body under general regulation, independent of whether fish are present.
